question_text: Which of the following statements about ultraviolet (UV) radiation is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: UV radiation has a longer wavelength than visible light

options.label: B
options.text: UV radiation is visible to the human eye

options.label: C
options.text: UV radiation has a shorter wavelength than visible light and can damage skin

options.label: D
options.text: UV radiation is used to cook food in ovens

explanation: Ultraviolet radiation has shorter wavelengths and higher frequencies than visible light, making it invisible to our eyes. UV radiation can penetrate the skin and cause damage to cells, leading to sunburn and potentially skin cancer if exposure is too high.
